valuation_methodology.owner_cash_definition: FY2025 company-defined free cash flow less share-based compensation; fixed-asset purchases and related payable changes are deducted
valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Keep current operating engines, reinvestment economics, unit inventory NAV, milestone project options, whole-security returns, and reverse expectations separate. Every material component receives an evidence-linked range; do not average the views.

component_valuation.methodology_note: Every material component receives a low/base/high present-value estimate. Range width reflects evidence quality, realization timing, and commercial uncertainty; it is not a zero-value placeholder.

valuation.evidence: FY2025 oil and gas royalty revenue was $411.7M. TPL bears no drilling capital; the scenario driver is royalty revenue growth, after-tax owner-cash conversion, duration, and discount rate.
valuation.cross_check: Do not add undeveloped royalty inventory that is already contributing to the $411.7M producing run-rate.
valuation.assumption_summary: Capital-free royalty cash flow. Base: 4% revenue growth in years 1-5, 2.5% in years 6-10, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 22x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.

components.label: Existing surface, easement, and land-use operations

valuation.evidence: FY2025 easement and other surface-related revenue was approximately $78.2M. TPL's May 2026 presentation shows recurring monetization across the well lifecycle plus emerging pipeline, power, communications, and industrial uses.
valuation.cross_check: Named uncontracted corridors and data-center projects remain in separate option components.
valuation.assumption_summary: Base: 6% growth in years 1-5 and 3.5% thereafter, 65% after-tax owner-cash conversion, 20x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.

valuation.evidence: FY2025 produced-water royalty revenue was approximately $124M on 4.3M barrels per day of royalty volume, with essentially no direct operating expense or capital. Presented volume grew from 433 MBbl/d in 2018 to 4,292 MBbl/d in 2025.
valuation.cross_check: The base growth rate is far below the 2018-2025 historical volume CAGR and must be tested against basin oil volumes, water-oil ratios, AMI coverage, and realized royalty per barrel.
valuation.assumption_summary: Capital-free stream. Base: 12% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 5% thereafter, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 24x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.

valuation.cross_check: No debt is disclosed in the FY2025 balance-sheet bridge.

valuation.evidence: Water-sales revenue grew from approximately $55M in 2020 to $170M in 2025. TPL disclosed $0.50-$1.00/bbl pricing, $0.10-$0.20/bbl direct cost, 63%-75% quarterly gross margins, maintenance capital near 10% of sales, and growth capital tied to line-of-sight demand.
valuation.cross_check: The model charges growth investment explicitly; higher growth creates value only when the assumed incremental after-tax return on capital is earned.
valuation.assumption_summary: Base: 10% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 4% thereafter, 35% after-tax owner-cash margin after maintenance capital, 30% incremental after-tax ROIC, 18x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.

valuation.evidence: FY2025 net PP&E of $164.5M; this is an embedded floor cross-check and not additive value.
valuation.cross_check: The operated-water DCF already capitalizes returns on installed infrastructure.

valuation.evidence: Only the risked present value of disclosed contracted economics is included here.
valuation.cross_check: Uncontracted data-center and power development remains in the separate project option.

valuation.evidence: TPL's 2024-2025 acquisitions printed near $19.8k, $26.0k, and $34.1k per NRA. Developed public royalty transactions, including Viper/Sitio, printed materially higher. The complete 224,000-NRA portfolio is marked at $25k/$40k/$75k per NRA, then the producing-royalty DCF is subtracted before allocating the residual between visible and dormant inventory.
valuation.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory reconcile to the total comparable value of all 224,000 NRA, so the same royalty acre cannot be capitalized twice.
valuation.assumption_summary: 45% of the comparable-NAV residual after subtracting producing royalty value; the 70,000-NRA label remains provisional until a tract-level inventory roll-forward is disclosed.

valuation.evidence: This is the remaining 55% of the total 224,000-NRA comparable-value residual after deducting the producing-royalty DCF. It recognizes dormant acreage without pretending that every acre is currently producing or independently saleable.
valuation.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory equal approximately $81/$130/$243 per share, corresponding to $25k/$40k/$75k per total NRA.
valuation.assumption_summary: 55% of the comparable-NAV residual; duration and tract-quality uncertainty are expressed through the portfolio unit marks rather than an arbitrary zero value.

valuation.evidence: TPL owns approximately 882,000 surface acres. TPL's May 2025 sale printed near $5,718/acre, LandBridge's recent fee-land transactions ranged from roughly $0.9k to $7.1k/acre, and Intrepid South Ranch sold with water and related assets for $70M. Portfolio marks remain below the best small-parcel and strategic-infrastructure transactions.
valuation.cross_check: Per-acre marks represent only residual value after recurring operations and named project options; they are not a second capitalization of existing easement cash flow.
valuation.assumption_summary: 882,000 acres at $1,500/$3,000/$5,718 per acre less 20%/15%/10% portfolio realization friction.

valuation.evidence: Only incremental, uncontracted pipeline, transmission, road, fiber, and logistics opportunities are included.
valuation.cross_check: Each project graduates into existing surface/easement operations once contracted.
valuation.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted successful-project PV less remaining development cost.

components.label: Data-center, power, and water co-location option

valuation.evidence: TPL disclosed a $50M Bolt investment, active LOIs/RFPs, site diligence, turbine and infrastructure work, and potential gigawatt-scale projects. These are not yet treated as contracted cash flow.
valuation.cross_check: The separately modeled receivable and the balance-sheet value of the Bolt investment are excluded from this incremental option.
valuation.assumption_summary: Success value is probability-weighted for customer, power, interconnection, water, capital, permitting, and commissioning milestones.

valuation.evidence: Phase 1 achieved strong water-quality results; the approximately 10,000-bbl/day Phase 2 facility was completed for May 2026 operation. Phase 3 at approximately 100,000 bbl/day remains subject to Phase 2 economics and FID.
valuation.cross_check: Commercial value requires repeatable unit economics and a capital-light license, cost-plus, JV, or spin structure; technical success alone is insufficient.
valuation.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted platform value less remaining commercialization cost; the asset state is demonstration, not research-only.

valuation.evidence: Reserve for taxes, transaction costs, and corporate friction when latent land and royalty value is realized; no debt is separately deducted.
valuation.cross_check: Low case absorbs more of the gross option value as realization cost.

inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 company-defined FCF $498.333M less $13.817M share-based compensation = $484.516M / 69.027M diluted shares (10-K FY2025)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: GAAP book excludes Assigned 1888 land and royalty interests (zero on balance sheet); not a fair-value or dhando floor
inputs.normalization_note: Owner cash deducts $59.531M fixed-asset purchases, $6.417M increase in related payables, and $13.817M share-based compensation. Acquisitions and the $50M equity-method investment are excluded from recurring owner cash.

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: The driver-based component base includes current royalty, surface, and water cash engines plus risked unit inventory and milestone options. It is a modeled value, not a liquidation guarantee.
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: Legacy $210.5 estimate retired because it mixed operating value with thinly supported acreage extrapolations
optionality_gate.notes: Operating return remains the stance gate. The component schedule separately estimates every material asset and reserve.

nav_overlay.retirement_note: Legacy illustrative marks retained for audit history only. They do not enter valuation_views or the stance gate.

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: 10-K FY2025: fair market value not determined in 1888; no value assigned to Assigned land and royalty interests
gaap_vs_fair_value.stockholders_equity_note: Mostly accumulated operating earnings; not land mark-to-market

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Water infrastructure replacement cost vs PPE net $164.5M — book used in SOTP; RC uplift [HUMAN REVIEW]
Full NRA roll-up separating producing vs undeveloped acres — marginal comp only

mar_2025_nra_acquisition.caveat: Marginal proved/acquired NRA only; assigned 1888 interests still at zero on BS

may_2025_land_sale.caveat: Single transaction; not representative of full 882k surface portfolio

segments_or_options.label: Undeveloped / incremental NRA royalty option
segments_or_options.evidence: 224k NRA × $19,775 Mar 2025 comp; P=25% for incremental drilling not in FY2025 run-rate

sotp_build.double_count_note: Producing royalties/easements/water are in segment PV; NRA comp and surface are incremental asset marks — do not add segment PV + full NRA comp without adjustment
sotp_build.tie_out: Price $393 vs overlay base ~$211/sh → market embeds Permian scarcity premium and growth beyond filing-backed marks; Lawrence base -1.0% on current OCF remains stance gate.

segment_build.allocation_method: FY2025 segment net income plus segment D&A, less fixed-asset purchases allocated to Water; $6.555M tax-timing reconciliation held separately

segments.label: Land & Resource (royalties + easements + land sales)
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 LRM net income $322.415M + D&A $44.555M = $366.970M / 69.027M shares; royalty acquisitions excluded
segments.notes: FY2025 segment $490.7M: royalties $411.7M, easements $78.2M; cyclical Permian toll

segments.label: Water Services and Operations (TPWR)
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 WSO net income $158.961M + D&A $17.978M - fixed-asset purchases/payable change $65.948M = $110.991M / 69.027M shares
segments.notes: FY2025 Water $307.5M (+16% YoY); embedded_in_segment for water ramp — not separate zero terminal

options.label: Undeveloped NRA / 1888 Assigned acreage option
options.base_terminal_rationale: Producing royalties in Land segment; incremental undeveloped drilling valued at P=25% × NRA comp — not in Lawrence 5% growth
options.evidence: 10-K: Assigned land/RRA at zero on BS; Mar 2025 177 NRA @ $3.5M (~$19,775/NRA)

options.label: Surface acreage (882k acres)
options.base_terminal_rationale: Surface rights separate from royalty cash flow; $2,500/ac [Assumption] vs May 2025 sale $5,718/ac

reconciliation.slack_notes: Segment cross-check excludes the small consolidated tax-timing reconciliation; no asset-option value is included.

bear.notes: Permian activity weakens; water growth slows; year-7 exit multiple compresses

base.notes: Low-to-mid-single-digit owner-cash growth; water and easements offset slower royalties; year-7 exit

bull.notes: Permian volumes and water infrastructure outperform; premium year-7 toll multiple sustained

growth_explanation.theory_label: Permian producing toll + water infra ramp (undeveloped NRA excluded)
growth_explanation.problem_situation: Lawrence base IRR -1.0% at $393 on $7.91/sh OCF. Why 5% years 1-5 when FY2025 OCF grew ~11% and Q1 rev +21%?

mechanisms.chain: Permian production on TPL acreage × royalty rates → $411.7M FY2025 royalties (+10% YoY) → operating cash grows low-to-mid single digits if activity holds
TPWR water sourcing/treatment volumes on scarce acreage → $307.5M FY2025 water revenue (+16% YoY) → lifts consolidated cash above royalty-only path
Long-dated easements with CPI escalators → $78.2M FY2025 (+24% YoY) adds stable mid-single-digit cash contribution

risky_predictions.text: Water segment revenue exceeds $330M in FY2026 if 5% consolidated cash path holds
Q2–Q4 2026 consolidated revenue growth stays above 10% YoY
risky_predictions.falsifier_action: Revise growth down; activity cycle weaker than base

falsifiers.observation: Permian rig count or completion intensity on TPL counties down >15% YoY for four consecutive quarters
falsifiers.action: Cut years 1–5 growth to 2%; move toward bear case

falsifiers.observation: Water revenue growth below 8% for two consecutive quarters while royalties flat
falsifiers.action: Remove water uplift from theory; cut consolidated to 3%

falsifiers.observation: FY2026 operating cash flow below $550M (~$7.95/sh) with no one-off explanation
falsifiers.action: Falsifies 5% path; revisit base at 2% or bear

ad_hoc_rescue_banned: Raising growth to match Q1 +21% revenue burst without Permian activity bridge
Using GAAP book $21/sh as downside floor
Smuggling undeveloped-acreage NAV into consolidated FCF growth

theory_implied.derivation: segment_build owner-cash weighted blend (growth only; exit from scenarios.base)

option_scan.question: In-business option: Undeveloped NRA / 1888 Assigned acreage option?
option_scan.evidence: 10-K: Assigned land/RRA at zero on BS; Mar 2025 177 NRA @ $3.5M (~$19,775/NRA)

synthesis.reason: Correlated operating paths and unsupported asset options must not be averaged into a consensus return.

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

themes.label: AI compute -> power demand -> grid/water constraint -> scarce Permian surface

indicators.label: TPL water segment revenue (USD m, FY)

peer_context.note: Context only; percentile rank not in base IRR.

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory persona context only. It does not replace the operating stance gate or enter valuation_views.

total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Cash totals, special-dividend classification, and both splits reconciled to TPL releases and SEC filings; ex-dates cross-checked to the market-data event feed. The plot begins in 2020 because earlier primary-source event coverage has not yet been established.

valuation_views.decision_rule: Do not average operating returns, component value, and reverse expectations.

components.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

reverse_expectations.warning: Required growth is a diagnostic, not a forecast.

legacy_output_policy.results_lawrence_legacy: compatibility alias of the current operating scenarios; not a separate valuation
legacy_output_policy.growth_explanation: qualitative scenario context only; legacy numerical paths do not enter the decision
legacy_output_policy.nav_overlay: audit history only when decision_use is false
legacy_output_policy.option_scan: inventory disclosure only; asset value enters only through valuation_views.assets

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Producing oil and gas royalties

pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: One post-December-2025-split common share has the same economic claim as every other common share. Q1 2026 diluted shares were approximately 69.0 million; TPL had net cash and an undrawn credit facility.
pricing_analysis.implied_growth_contract: Constant 7-year owner-cash growth with a 20x terminal owner-cash multiple; diagnostic, not forecast.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: The component model may still understate repeated monetization of the same acreage, increasing water intensity, and data-center or power demand that converts faster than the milestone probabilities assume.
falsifiers: Contracted data-center and power economics support materially more than the current option marks without TPL-funded capital.
Produced-water royalties sustain growth above the base case while realized revenue per barrel remains stable.
Operated-water investment cohorts demonstrate incremental after-tax ROIC materially above 30%.
pricing_analysis.pricing_conclusion: TPL is an exceptional asset system. After marking its complete royalty acreage to actual royalty transactions and raising the surface-land cross-check, the quote is above the risked base value but below the high economic case. The market still requires strong royalty, produced-water, operated-water, and strategic-project execution, so the security remains a watch rather than a rejection of the asset quality.

economic_value_bridge.gaap_role: Reference only. Assigned perpetual royalty interests remain at zero GAAP carrying value and surface land is held far below economic value; neither figure is used as the valuation floor.
economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: TPL's 2025 10-K reports approximately $840M net acquired royalty interests, while the legacy 1/16 and 1/128 perpetual interests remain at zero carrying value.

component_groups.label: Producing plus undeveloped royalty estate
component_groups.valuation_basis: 224,000 total NRA at $25k/$40k/$75k per NRA; TPL acquisition marks near $19.8k/$26.0k/$34.1k and developed public royalty transactions provide the ladder.
component_groups.overlap_control: The producing DCF is subtracted from total royalty NAV before visible and dormant residual values are added.

component_groups.label: Surface land, existing uses, and future corridors
component_groups.valuation_basis: Existing cash-flow value plus 882,000-acre portfolio marks anchored to TPL, LandBridge, and Intrepid transactions.
component_groups.overlap_control: Existing contracted uses, residual land, and uncontracted corridors are separate economic claims.

component_groups.label: Produced-water royalty, operated water, and technology
component_groups.valuation_basis: Capital-free produced-water royalties are separated from capital-requiring operated water and stage-gated treatment technology.
component_groups.overlap_control: Royalty volume, operated margin, and technology licensing are modeled separately; infrastructure replacement cost remains embedded.

component_groups.label: Cash, investments, and contracted receivable
component_groups.overlap_control: The Bolt investment is excluded from the incremental project success value.

component_groups.label: Data-center, power, and water co-location option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Milestone probability × success value less remaining TPL capital; powered-land scarcity is context, not a contracted rent assumption.
component_groups.overlap_control: Only uncontracted incremental project economics are included.

component_groups.label: Corporate and realization reserve
component_groups.overlap_control: Applied once at the consolidated level.

wisdom_applied: Horizon Kinetics: value the capital-free royalty structure and dormant assets, not their GAAP carrying amount.
Horizon Kinetics/PrairieSky: third parties fund drilling, so acreage content and cash flow can expand without owner capital.
Groundbreaker: the same non-depleting surface can host sequential tolls, but each toll is separated to prevent double counting.
Klarman: current cash flows, comparable asset NAV, and uncontracted catalysts remain distinct layers.
limitations: TPL does not disclose a tract-level split between producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant NRA; the residual reconciliation is portfolio-level.

Royalty transaction values vary dramatically with production, operator, location, and inventory depth; the $75k high mark remains below developed Viper/Sitio marks but above TPL's recent acquisition marks.
Data-center and desalination success values are not comparable NAV and remain probability weighted.

economic_claim.description: One post-December-2025-split common share has the same economic claim as every other common share. Q1 2026 diluted shares were approximately 69.0 million; TPL had net cash and an undrawn credit facility.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 oil and gas royalty revenue was $411.7M. TPL bears no drilling capital; the scenario driver is royalty revenue growth, after-tax owner-cash conversion, duration, and discount rate.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The producing DCF is subtracted from total royalty NAV before visible and dormant residual values are added.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Surface land, existing uses, and future corridors
valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 easement and other surface-related revenue was approximately $78.2M. TPL's May 2026 presentation shows recurring monetization across the well lifecycle plus emerging pipeline, power, communications, and industrial uses.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Existing contracted uses, residual land, and uncontracted corridors are separate economic claims.
valuation_proof.falsifier: A directly comparable transaction or asset-specific impairment supports a value below the low-case unit mark after like-for-like adjustments.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Produced-water royalty, operated water, and technology
valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 produced-water royalty revenue was approximately $124M on 4.3M barrels per day of royalty volume, with essentially no direct operating expense or capital. Presented volume grew from 433 MBbl/d in 2018 to 4,292 MBbl/d in 2025.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Royalty volume, operated margin, and technology licensing are modeled separately; infrastructure replacement cost remains embedded.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The Bolt investment is excluded from the incremental project success value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Produced-water royalty, operated water, and technology
valuation_proof.evidence: Water-sales revenue grew from approximately $55M in 2020 to $170M in 2025. TPL disclosed $0.50-$1.00/bbl pricing, $0.10-$0.20/bbl direct cost, 63%-75% quarterly gross margins, maintenance capital near 10% of sales, and growth capital tied to line-of-sight demand.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Royalty volume, operated margin, and technology licensing are modeled separately; infrastructure replacement cost remains embedded.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

valuation_proof.evidence: Only the risked present value of disclosed contracted economics is included here.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The Bolt investment is excluded from the incremental project success value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation_proof.evidence: TPL's 2024-2025 acquisitions printed near $19.8k, $26.0k, and $34.1k per NRA. Developed public royalty transactions, including Viper/Sitio, printed materially higher. The complete 224,000-NRA portfolio is marked at $25k/$40k/$75k per NRA, then the producing-royalty DCF is subtracted before allocating the residual between visible and dormant inventory.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The producing DCF is subtracted from total royalty NAV before visible and dormant residual values are added.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

valuation_proof.evidence: This is the remaining 55% of the total 224,000-NRA comparable-value residual after deducting the producing-royalty DCF. It recognizes dormant acreage without pretending that every acre is currently producing or independently saleable.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The producing DCF is subtracted from total royalty NAV before visible and dormant residual values are added.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Surface land, existing uses, and future corridors
valuation_proof.evidence: TPL owns approximately 882,000 surface acres. TPL's May 2025 sale printed near $5,718/acre, LandBridge's recent fee-land transactions ranged from roughly $0.9k to $7.1k/acre, and Intrepid South Ranch sold with water and related assets for $70M. Portfolio marks remain below the best small-parcel and strategic-infrastructure transactions.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Existing contracted uses, residual land, and uncontracted corridors are separate economic claims.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Surface land, existing uses, and future corridors
valuation_proof.evidence: Only incremental, uncontracted pipeline, transmission, road, fiber, and logistics opportunities are included.

valuation_proof.evidence: TPL disclosed a $50M Bolt investment, active LOIs/RFPs, site diligence, turbine and infrastructure work, and potential gigawatt-scale projects. These are not yet treated as contracted cash flow.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Produced-water royalty, operated water, and technology
valuation_proof.evidence: Phase 1 achieved strong water-quality results; the approximately 10,000-bbl/day Phase 2 facility was completed for May 2026 operation. Phase 3 at approximately 100,000 bbl/day remains subject to Phase 2 economics and FID.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Royalty volume, operated margin, and technology licensing are modeled separately; infrastructure replacement cost remains embedded.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

valuation_proof.evidence: Reserve for taxes, transaction costs, and corporate friction when latent land and royalty value is realized; no debt is separately deducted.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Applied once at the consolidated level.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The obligation, financing claim, tax, or realization friction is materially larger than the low-case reserve.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 net PP&E of $164.5M; this is an embedded floor cross-check and not additive value.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The operated-water DCF already capitalizes returns on installed infrastructure.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.
